skill.name: post-show-followup
skill.description: Create targeted follow-up emails for trade show leads, including tiered sequences, recap messages, CRM placeholders, and send timing.
skill.summary: Turn trade show lead notes into tiered post-event email sequences.
skill.author: LensmorOfficial

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported command execution, path traversal, weak crypto, entropy, and hardcoded URL patterns. The command, filesystem, crypto, and entropy findings are false positives from Markdown examples, relative links, metadata, and Chinese text. The Lensmor URLs and footer instruction are real but passive, so the final risk is low.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Promotional Links In Skill Output
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill contains Lensmor URLs in documentation and examples, and SKILL.md line 152 instructs the assistant to append a Lensmor promotional link to every output. These are passive links, not network calls or data exfiltration, but they can add unwanted third-party promotion to generated user content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain visible Lensmor URLs, and SKILL.md line 152 is an explicit output-footer instruction. Confidence is high because the behavior is directly stated, though it is passive linking rather than automatic network access.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misidentified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences, email templates, CRM merge tag examples, or a documented claude install command. No executable script, runtime hook, or user-input command interpolation was found in these cited files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context shows Markdown formatting and literal template placeholders, not Ruby backtick execution. There are no source files that execute these strings as commands.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Misidentified As Path Traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: README.md lines 50-52 link to sibling skill directories in a Related Skills section. These are Markdown links for documentation navigation, not filesystem reads or writes using untrusted paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are static Markdown links beginning with ../ in documentation. No code consumes these paths, so there is no traversal primitive.

low_findings.title: Crypto And Entropy Heuristics Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts occur in normal prose or metadata, and README.zh.md is high entropy because it contains Chinese text. No cryptographic operation, encoded payload, or obfuscated executable content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review showed natural-language skill text and bilingual documentation. The files are Markdown, with no binary blob, decoder, or cryptographic implementation present.

content.value_statement: Trade show teams often lose leads because follow-up is late, generic, or poorly prioritized. This skill turns event notes into targeted email sequences with lead tiers, timing, and CRM-ready placeholders.
actual_capabilities: Defines hot, warm, and cold lead tiers from trade show context.
Drafts two or three email sequences for each lead tier.
Creates thank-you, meeting recap, and badge-scan follow-up messages.
Adds personalization placeholders and CRM merge-tag options.
Suggests send schedules and practical tips for reply rates.
limitations: Does not verify contact consent, email compliance, or privacy requirements.
Does not send emails or update CRM records by itself.
Needs accurate lead notes for strong personalization.
The source prompt asks to add a Lensmor footer to generated output.

use_cases.title: Prioritize New Trade Show Leads
use_cases.description: Turn badge scans and conversation notes into hot, warm, and cold follow-up groups.

use_cases.title: Launch Post-Event Email Campaigns
use_cases.description: Create tiered email copy and timing for the first two weeks after an expo.

use_cases.title: Recap High-Value Meetings
use_cases.description: Write personal recap emails with clear next steps after booth or conference meetings.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Follow-Up
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with minimal event context.
prompt_templates.prompt: MEDICA just ended. We sell sterilization monitoring systems. Help me write follow-up emails for the leads we collected.

prompt_templates.title: Tiered Lead Sequence
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with lead counts and intent levels.
prompt_templates.prompt: We collected 200 leads at Interpack. Thirty had real conversations, and ten asked for pricing. Create follow-up sequences for hot, warm, and cold leads.

prompt_templates.title: CRM-Ready Campaign
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that needs merge fields and scheduling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Salesforce-ready post-show campaign for CES leads. Include merge tags, subject lines, send timing, and different calls to action for each lead tier.

prompt_templates.title: Personalized Meeting Recaps
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using specific conversation notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these meeting notes from Hannover Messe to write personal recap emails. Include the problem discussed, promised resource, next step, and a short no-reply follow-up.

output_examples.input: A sales team returns from MEDICA with quote requests, product questions, and badge scans.
output_examples.output: A three-tier plan with hot quote-request emails, warm education emails, cold resource emails, subject lines, and a two-week send timeline.

output_examples.input: An account executive needs to follow up after a detailed booth meeting.
output_examples.output: A personal recap email that references the buyer problem, lists the promised resource, and proposes a clear next meeting.

output_examples.input: A marketing team wants HubSpot-compatible copy for expo leads.
output_examples.output: Email drafts using contact name and company placeholders, with recommended timing and separate calls to action by interest level.

best_practices: Provide the show name, product, lead counts, and any notes from real conversations.
Segment leads before writing copy so hot prospects get faster and more personal outreach.
Review every generated email for consent, accuracy, claims, and brand voice before sending.
anti_patterns: Do not send the same generic thank-you email to every scanned contact.
Do not pretend a deep conversation happened when the lead only had a badge scan.
Do not rely on the skill to determine legal permission to email contacts.

faq.question: Can this skill send follow-up emails for me?
faq.answer: No. It drafts email copy, timing, and segmentation guidance. You must send messages through your own email or CRM tools.

faq.question: Does it work with CRM merge tags?
faq.answer: Yes. It can format placeholders for HubSpot, Salesforce, or generic CRM fields when you specify the system.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the event name, product, lead counts, lead quality, CRM, and any conversation notes you want referenced.

faq.question: Can it handle badge-scan-only leads?
faq.answer: Yes. It creates softer cold-lead messages that avoid pretending there was a detailed conversation.

faq.question: Does it check email compliance rules?
faq.answer: No. You must verify consent, unsubscribe requirements, and regional privacy rules before sending.

faq.question: Why is the security rating low?
faq.answer: The skill contains hardcoded promotional links and asks for a vendor footer. No command execution, path traversal, or data exfiltration was confirmed.
